definitions: Louis-Napoleon Bonaparte, the first President of France (1848‚Äď52) and the last French monarch (as Emperor of the French, 1852‚Äď70).
A style of furniture and decorative arts popular during his reign, characterized by a revival of earlier styles and opulent ornamentation.
examples: Napoleon III was the nephew of Napoleon I.
The Second French Empire under Napoleon III saw significant industrialization.
Many pieces from the Napoleon III era are highly sought after by collectors.
